html_body:
QSC Core Health Analysis Report

Core: eternia-Core24-d447
Analysis Period: 2026-06-02 15:46:39 UTC to 2026-06-03 15:46:39 UTC (24 hours)
Report Generated: 2026-06-03 21:16 IST

1. CORE IDENTITY

Core Instance: eternia-Core24-d447
Location: QSC Sleestack Platform
Monitored Processes: audio_engine, runtime_engine, video_engine, node, engine_discover
Data Sources: Prometheus (grafanacloud-prom), Loki (grafanacloud-logs)

2. SYSTEM RESOURCE SUMMARY

ResourceCurrent State24h PeakStatus
Total CPU Utilization46.5%~50%HEALTHY
Total Memory Utilization6.2%~10%HEALTHY
Active Processes10 instances18 total instancesWARNING
Process Restarts (24h)18 restartsaudio_engine: 5, runtime_engine: 5CRITICAL

Aggregate CPU usage at 46.5% with headroom. Memory utilization low at 6.2%. Critical concern: 18 process restarts indicate systemic instability.

3. CPU STATE BREAKDOWN

ProcessUser CPUSystem CPUTotal CPU24h PeakStatus
audio_engine31.8%5.2%37.0%35.9%WARNING
runtime_engine4.2%1.2%5.4%6.0%HEALTHY
node3.1%0.7%4.1%10.6%HEALTHY
video_engine0.03%0.01%0.04%0.05%HEALTHY
engine_discover0.13%0.03%0.15%0.2%HEALTHY
  • audio_engine dominates CPU: 37% current, peak 35.9% at 15:37 UTC on 2026-06-03. Sustained high demand with user-mode accounting for 86% of utilization.
  • No I/O wait states detected: All processes show 0% wait-state, indicating CPU-bound workloads with no I/O blocking.
  • Multiple audio_engine restarts: 5 distinct PIDs detected (6077, 4652, 25355, 2066, 14792) suggesting crash/restart cycles every 6-8 hours.
  • System CPU overhead minimal: System-mode CPU averages 0.7% across all processes; kernel interaction efficient.

4. MEMORY STATE BREAKDOWN

ProcessCurrent Memory %Current Memory (MB)24h Peak (MB)Status
runtime_engine2.56%409.8414.1HEALTHY
node1.54%246.3756.7WARNING
audio_engine1.49%238.5239.0HEALTHY
video_engine0.51%74.5749.0CRITICAL
engine_discover0.078%149.4149.4HEALTHY
  • node process shows highest absolute memory: Peak 756.7 MB (4.72%) at 15:05 UTC, currently 246.3 MB due to recent restart.
  • runtime_engine stable at 409.8 MB: Longest-running instance (PID 6688) shows minimal variance over 22.43h.
  • video_engine catastrophic drop: Severe degradation from 749.0 MB to 42.0 MB detected at 15:15 UTC, indicating crash or process restart.
  • engine_discover gradual growth: +7.6% increase over 24h (138.9 MB → 149.4 MB), consistent low-rate accumulation.

5. PROCESS-LEVEL ANALYSIS

ProcessPIDUptimeCPU %Memory (MB)ThreadsStatus
audio_engine1479232.7 min31.8%238.512WARNING
audio_engine607722.4h—239.012HEALTHY
runtime_engine1546432 min4.2%409.8197HEALTHY
runtime_engine668822.4h—409.7204WARNING
node892932 min3.1%246.313HEALTHY
node900023.5h—739.713WARNING
video_engine987923.5h0.03%42.063→56CRITICAL
video_engine978932 min—74.562HEALTHY
engine_discover1059623.5h0.13%149.41HEALTHY
engine_discover1050632 min——1HEALTHY
  • Dual process pattern observed: All process types maintain old long-running instances (22-23h) plus new instances (~32 min), indicating controlled refresh strategy.
  • audio_engine maintains 12 threads consistently: No thread leak detected across all 5 instances.
  • runtime_engine shows elevated thread count (PID 6688): 204 threads vs. 197-199 average, potential thread accumulation worth monitoring.
  • video_engine thread decline: PID 9879 threads decreased from 63 to 56 over 24h, may indicate degradation preceding memory crash.

6. MEMORY LEAK DETECTION

Process (PID)Start Memory (MB)End Memory (MB)GrowthDurationLeak Risk
runtime_engine (15464)395.7409.8+14.1 MB0.52hMODERATE
engine_discover (10596)138.9149.4+10.5 MB23.5hLOW
node (8929)239.7246.3+6.6 MB0.52hLOW
node (9000)714.9739.7+24.8 MB23.5hLOW
audio_engine (all)——0 MB—NONE
video_engine (9879)749.042.0-707 MB23.5hCRASH
  • runtime_engine (PID 15464) shows steepest recent growth: +14.1 MB in 32 minutes (27 MB/hour rate). Requires extended monitoring to confirm leak.
  • engine_discover exhibits slow steady accumulation: +7.6% over 24h, consistent low-rate pattern, not concerning for short-term.
  • No acute leak in audio_engine: All instances show flat memory profiles with zero variance.
  • video_engine memory crash confirmed: Catastrophic 707 MB drop indicates process failure at ~15:15 UTC.

7. CRASH AND STACKTRACE ANALYSIS

Fatal Error Count: 0 errors detected in 24-hour period
Stacktrace Logs: No fatal/error/crash/panic logs found in Loki for service_name='stacktrace', instance='eternia-Core24-d447'

⚠️ WARNING: Despite 18 process restarts detected in metrics, NO corresponding stacktrace logs were found. This indicates either:

  • Graceful process recycling (planned restarts)
  • Silent crashes without logging
  • Incomplete log collection/forwarding
  • Stacktrace logging configuration issue

Recommendation: Verify stacktrace logging is enabled and logs are being forwarded properly. The absence of crash logs despite multiple restarts is concerning.

8. METRICS AND LOG CORRELATION

Timestamp (UTC)EventMetric EvidenceLog Evidence
2026-06-03 14:09-14:55Coordinated restart clusteraudio_engine (PID 25355), runtime_engine (PID 25947, 5270) restarts within 46-min windowNo logs
2026-06-03 15:15video_engine crashMemory drop 749.0 MB → 42.0 MB, thread decline 63→56No logs
2026-06-03 15:16audio_engine restartNew PID 14792 started, now running 32.7 minNo logs
2026-06-03 15:37audio_engine CPU spikePeak 35.9% user CPU on PID 14792No logs

Gap Analysis: Zero correlation between restart metrics and log events. This logging blind spot prevents root cause analysis. Cannot determine if restarts are crashes, OOM kills, or controlled recycling without log evidence.

9. UPTIME AND RESTART ANALYSIS

ProcessRestart Count (24h)Longest UptimeShortest UptimeStability Score
audio_engine522.4h (PID 6077)16 minCRITICAL
runtime_engine522.4h (PID 6688)16 minCRITICAL
video_engine423.5h (PID 9879)17 minWARNING
node223.5h (PID 9000)32 minHEALTHY
engine_discover223.5h (PID 10596)32 minHEALTHY
  • Total restarts: 18 across 5 process types
  • Critical instability in audio_engine and runtime_engine: 5 restarts each indicate potential crash loops or resource exhaustion.
  • Restart cluster at 14:09-14:55 UTC: Suggests coordinated system intervention or cascading failure.
  • Recent restart wave at ~15:14-15:16 UTC: Multiple processes restarted simultaneously, creating new ~32-min-uptime instances.

10. ANOMALIES

  1. 🔴 video_engine memory crash (15:15 UTC): Catastrophic drop from 749 MB to 42 MB without stacktrace logs. Thread count also declined 63→56. Process failed silently.
  2. 🟠 18 process restarts with zero crash logs: Severe logging gap prevents root cause analysis. Cannot distinguish crashes from controlled recycling.
  3. 🟠 audio_engine CPU volatility: Rapid swing from 10.4% (valley at 15:35) to 35.9% peak (15:37) within 2 minutes indicates burst processing pattern or workload instability.
  4. 🟠 Coordinated restart cluster (14:09-14:55): Multiple processes restarted within 46-minute window suggests system-wide intervention or cascading resource exhaustion.
  5. 🟠 runtime_engine thread count variance: PID 6688 shows 204 threads vs. 197-199 baseline in other instances, potential thread accumulation.
  6. 🟡 Dual process lifecycle pattern: All process types maintain old (22-23h) + new (~32 min) instances simultaneously. Controlled refresh or failover strategy?
  7. 🟡 node memory consumption variation: PID 9000 peaked at 756.7 MB while PID 8929 uses only 246.3 MB. Same process type, 3x memory difference.

11. SUMMARY AND RISK ASSESSMENT

OVERALL STATUS: ⚠️ WARNING (Trending toward CRITICAL)

Critical Risks:

  • 🔴 High restart frequency: 18 process restarts in 24h (audio_engine: 5, runtime_engine: 5, video_engine: 4) indicates systemic instability.
  • 🔴 video_engine silent crash: 707 MB memory loss without logging suggests silent failure mode.
  • 🔴 Zero crash logs despite multiple restarts: Logging blind spot prevents root cause diagnosis and future incident prevention.

Moderate Risks:

  • 🟠 audio_engine sustained high CPU: 37% utilization with burst spikes to 35.9%. Process is resource-intensive with restart history.
  • 🟠 Potential memory leak in runtime_engine (PID 15464): +14.1 MB in 32 minutes requires extended monitoring.
  • 🟠 Thread count anomaly in runtime_engine (PID 6688): 204 threads vs. 197-199 baseline.

Positive Observations:

  • ✅ System resource headroom adequate: 46.5% CPU, 6.2% memory with capacity available
  • ✅ No I/O bottlenecks: Zero wait-state CPU indicates healthy storage/network
  • ✅ audio_engine shows no memory leak: Flat memory profile across all instances
  • ✅ engine_discover and node processes relatively stable (2 restarts each)

12. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS

🔴 IMMEDIATE (Next 1-4 hours):

  1. Verify and fix stacktrace logging: Confirm logs are being generated, forwarded, and indexed. Without crash logs, you're flying blind.
  2. Investigate video_engine crash root cause: Examine host logs, dmesg, OOM killer, kernel logs around 15:15 UTC on 2026-06-03.
  3. Monitor runtime_engine (PID 15464) memory growth: Extended observation needed to confirm if +27 MB/hour rate continues (potential leak).

🟠 SHORT-TERM (Next 24-48 hours):

  1. Root cause analysis for audio_engine restarts: 5 restarts in 24h unacceptable. Check for OOM kills, segfaults, resource limits, watchdog timeouts.
  2. Establish restart baseline and alerting: Define acceptable restart frequency threshold and alert when exceeded.
  3. Analyze coordinated restart cluster (14:09-14:55): Determine if system intervention, cascading failure, or external trigger caused simultaneous restarts.
  4. Investigate audio_engine CPU burst pattern: 10.4% → 35.9% within 2 minutes. Is this normal workload or pathological behavior?

🟡 MEDIUM-TERM (Next 1-2 weeks):

  1. Review dual process lifecycle strategy: Clarify if old+new simultaneous instances are intentional (blue/green, canary) or symptom of incomplete shutdown.
  2. Establish memory growth baselines per process: Define normal growth rates and alert thresholds for leak detection.
  3. Implement comprehensive health checks: Liveness, readiness probes for each process type with automatic restart policies.
  4. Capacity planning: While current headroom exists (46.5% CPU, 6.2% memory), plan for peak load scenarios given audio_engine's high utilization.
  5. Thread count monitoring for runtime_engine: Alert on PID-to-PID variance >5% to catch thread accumulation early.

Report generated by QSC Core Health Analysis Agent | Data sources: Prometheus (grafanacloud-prom), Loki (grafanacloud-logs) | Analysis period: 2026-06-02 15:46:39 UTC to 2026-06-03 15:46:39 UTC
